description: HP/Agilent 53310A Modulation Domain Analyzer. Measures frequency, time-interval and phase vs time and builds histograms. Programmed via SCPI 1990.0 over HP-IB (GPIB). Two input channels (A=1, B=2) plus optional C channel.

commands.name: calibration_query
commands.description: Performs an internal interpolator self-calibration and returns a value indicating success.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the status data structures (event registers and error queue).

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the Standard Event Status Enable Register.

commands.name: event_status_register_query
commands.description: Queries the Standard Event Status Register.

commands.name: identification_query
commands.description: Queries the analyzer identification (manufacturer, model, serial, firmware).

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Sets the operation complete bit in the Standard Event Status Register when all pending operations finish.

commands.name: operation_complete_query
commands.description: Places an ASCII '1' in the Output Queue when all pending operations are completed.

commands.name: option_query
commands.description: Identifies the options installed in the analyzer.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Restores analyzer state from a copy stored in local non-volatile memory.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the analyzer to a known state.

commands.name: save
commands.description: Stores the current state of the analyzer in local non-volatile memory.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the Service Request Enable Register.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Queries the Status Byte and Master Summary Status bit.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Device-specific analog of IEEE 488.1 GET. Initiates a single measurement.

commands.name: self_test_query
commands.description: Executes an internal self-test and reports the results.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Makes the analyzer wait until all pending operations are complete before executing following commands.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Aborts the measurement in progress. Event; no query.

commands.name: configure_histogram_frequency
commands.description: Configures the analyzer for a frequency Fast Histogram measurement. Channel suffix 1=A, 2=B, 3=C.

commands.name: configure_histogram_tinterval
commands.description: Configures the analyzer for a time-interval Fast Histogram measurement.

commands.name: configure_xtime_frequency
commands.description: Configures the analyzer for a frequency vs Time measurement. Channel suffix 1=A, 2=B, 3=C.

commands.name: configure_xtime_tinterval
commands.description: Configures the analyzer for a time-interval vs Time measurement.

commands.name: configure_query
commands.description: Returns the function configured by the last :CONFigure or :MEASure command.

commands.name: fetch_scalar_result
commands.description: Fetches a single ASCII scalar analysis result (IMEan, IMRate, MAXimum, MEAN, MINimum, MRATe, PROBability, PTPeak, SDEViation) for the configured frequency or time-interval function. IMRate/MRATe require vs Time.

commands.name: fetch_histogram
commands.description: Fetches Histogram data (from vs Time or Fast Histogram depending on :CONFigure). Format determined by :FORMat.

commands.name: fetch_xtime
commands.description: Fetches vs Time measurement data. Format determined by :FORMat.

commands.name: fetch_query
commands.description: Retrieves the last function fetched, read, configured, or measured.

commands.name: measure_histogram_frequency
commands.description: Stops measuring, configures and makes a single frequency Fast Histogram measurement, placing data in the Output Queue. Channel 1=A, 2=B, 3=C.

commands.name: measure_histogram_tinterval
commands.description: Makes a single time-interval Fast Histogram measurement and returns the data.

commands.name: measure_xtime_frequency
commands.description: Makes a single frequency vs Time measurement and returns the data. Channel 1=A, 2=B, 3=C.

commands.name: measure_xtime_tinterval
commands.description: Makes a single time-interval vs Time measurement and returns the data.

commands.name: read_scalar_result
commands.description: Stops measuring, makes a single measurement and returns a scalar analysis result (IMEan, IMRate, MAXimum, MEAN, MINimum, MRATe, PROBability, PTPeak, SDEViation).

commands.name: read_histogram
commands.description: Makes a single measurement and returns Histogram data. Format determined by :FORMat.

commands.name: read_xtime
commands.description: Makes a single measurement and returns vs Time data. Format determined by :FORMat.

commands.name: read_query
commands.description: Retrieves the last function fetched, read, configured, or measured.

commands.name: calc_analysis_all_off
commands.description: Disables all analysis functions. Event; no query.

commands.name: calc_analysis_delimit
commands.description: Selects the analysis range: ALL data or between MARKers. Suffix 1=vs Time, 2=Histogram. *RST=ALL.

commands.name: calc_analysis_function_state
commands.description: Enables/disables an analysis function. Functions: IMEan, IMRate, MAXimum, MEAN, MINimum, MRATe, PROBability, PTPeak, SDEViation. Maximum 4 enabled at a time. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: calc_analysis_function_result
commands.description: Queries the result of an analysis function. The function's STATe must be ON.

commands.name: calc_data_query
commands.description: Returns the Histogram from vs Time when CALC:HIST is ON and sensing vs Time. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_encode_novalue
commands.description: Queries the NOValue encoding for the dimension. 1=Y-axis, 2=X-axis. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_format_query
commands.description: Queries the data format of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_label_query
commands.description: Queries the label of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_offset_query
commands.description: Queries the offset of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_scale_query
commands.description: Queries the scale of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_size_query
commands.description: Queries the number of points in the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_dimension_type_query
commands.description: Queries the type of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: calc_histogram_state
commands.description: ON displays Histogram from vs Time; OFF displays vs Time. Applies when sensing vs Time. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: calc_histogram_accumulate
commands.description: ON accumulates histogram data whether STATe is ON or OFF. Coupled to :SENSe:HISTogram:ACCumulate. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: calc_histogram_samples_above_query
commands.description: Returns the number of samples at or above RANGe:UPPer. Query only.

commands.name: calc_histogram_samples_below_query
commands.description: Returns the number of samples at or below RANGe:LOWer. Query only.

commands.name: calc_histogram_samples_in_query
commands.description: Returns the number of samples in the histogram. Query only.

commands.name: calc_immediate
commands.description: Recalculates without reacquiring data. Event; no query.

commands.name: calc_path_query
commands.description: Returns the calculation path; couples to SENSe:FUNCtion. Query only.

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Clears displayed data and invalidates the data available. Event; no query.

commands.name: diag_cal_input_gain_auto
commands.description: ONCE calibrates input gain for channel (1=A, 2=B). *RST=OFF.

commands.name: diag_cal_input_offset_auto
commands.description: ONCE calibrates input offset for channel (1=A, 2=B). *RST=OFF.

commands.name: diag_cal_interp_auto
commands.description: ONCE calibrates the interpolators. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: diag_cal_status_query
commands.description: Returns 0 for calibration completed successfully, 1 for calibration failed. Query only.

commands.name: display_trace_connect
commands.description: ON connects measured samples on the display. *RST=ON.

commands.name: display_trace_graticule_axis
commands.description: Axis graticule (vs Time only). ON couples FRAMe ON and GRID OFF. *RST=ON.

commands.name: display_trace_graticule_frame
commands.description: Frame graticule. ON couples AXIS OFF and GRID OFF. *RST=ON.

commands.name: display_trace_graticule_grid
commands.description: Grid graticule. ON couples AXIS ON and FRAMe ON. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: display_trace_persistence
commands.description: Display persistence. Applies when :SENSe:SWEep:TIME:REPetitive is OFF. *RST=SINGle.

commands.name: display_trace_probability_scale_auto
commands.description: ON scales the Histogram Y-axis display based on data instead of MIN and MAX. *RST=ON.

commands.name: display_trace_probability_scale_maximum
commands.description: Sets the maximum of the Histogram probability (Y-axis) scale. *RST=100.

commands.name: display_trace_probability_scale_minimum
commands.description: Sets the minimum of the Histogram probability (Y-axis) scale. *RST=0.

commands.name: display_trace_probability_spacing
commands.description: Sets the type of spacing used on the Histogram Y-axis display. *RST=LINear.

commands.name: display_menu
commands.description: Displays the selected front-panel menu. Event; no query.

commands.name: format_data
commands.description: Sets the data format for queries returning vs Time or Histogram data. BLOCk is 16-bit integers for vs Time and 64-bit reals for Histogram. *RST=ASCii.

commands.name: initiate_immediate
commands.description: Initiates a single measurement. Event; no query.

commands.name: initiate_continuous
commands.description: Enables/disables repetitive measurements. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: input_coupling
commands.description: Sets the input coupling for channel (1=A, 2=B). *RST=DC.

commands.name: input_impedance
commands.description: Sets the input impedance for channel (1=A, 2=B). Enter 50 OHM or 1 MOHM. *RST=1 MOHM.

commands.name: marker_frequency_state
commands.description: Enables/disables frequency markers. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: marker_frequency_position
commands.description: Sets the position of frequency marker 1 or 2. *RST: POS1=85 MHZ, POS2=115 MHZ.

commands.name: marker_frequency_position_auto
commands.description: AUTO ON makes frequency and TI markers track the last analysis function enabled. *RST=ON.

commands.name: marker_probability_state
commands.description: Enables/disables Histogram probability markers (Histogram Y-axis). *RST=OFF.

commands.name: marker_probability_position_query
commands.description: Queries the probability marker position (a function of the frequency or TI marker position). Query only.

commands.name: marker_time_state
commands.description: Enables/disables Time markers (vs Time X-axis). *RST=OFF.

commands.name: marker_time_position
commands.description: Sets the position of Time marker 1 or 2. *RST: POS1=-0.4E-3, POS2=+0.4E-3.

commands.name: marker_tinterval_state
commands.description: Enables/disables time interval markers. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: marker_tinterval_position
commands.description: Sets the position of TI marker 1 or 2. *RST: POS1=0.7E-3, POS2=1.3E-3.

commands.name: marker_tinterval_position_auto
commands.description: AUTO ON makes TI and frequency markers track the last analysis function enabled. *RST=ON.

commands.name: sense_data_query
commands.description: Returns vs Time or Fast Histogram data (uses :FORMat). Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_encode_novalue
commands.description: Queries the NOValue encoding for the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_format_query
commands.description: Queries the data format of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_label_query
commands.description: Queries the label of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_offset_query
commands.description: Queries the offset of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_scale_query
commands.description: Queries the scale of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_size_query
commands.description: Queries the number of points in the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_dimension_type_query
commands.description: Queries the type of the dimension. Query only.

commands.name: sense_event_hysteresis_relative
commands.description: Sets the input event hysteresis as 0-100% for channel (1=A, 2=B). *RST=0.

commands.name: sense_event_level
commands.description: Sets the input event (trigger) level voltage for channel (1=A, 2=B). *RST=0 V.

commands.name: sense_event_level_auto
commands.description: ONCE finds the 50% voltage threshold of the input signal. Event; with query. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_cfinterval
commands.description: Sampling interval at center frequency for ARM:SOURce AUTO. 1=A, 2=B, 3=C. *RST=5E-5.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_cfinterval_auto
commands.description: ON couples the interval to SWEep:TIME:SPAN. *RST=ON.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_ecount_query
commands.description: Returns the actual arm event count. 1=A, 2=B. Query only.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_ecount_multiplier
commands.description: Arm event count multiplier; applies when SOURce LINK and frequency A or B selected. 1=A, 2=B. *RST: ECO1=2, ECO2=1.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_level
commands.description: External arm level; applies when SOURce EXTernal, or SOURce LINK and frequency C selected. *RST=1.5 V.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_link_query
commands.description: Returns INT1, INT2, or EXT for frequency A, B, or C respectively. Query only.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_slope
commands.description: External arm slope; applies when SOURce EXTernal, or SOURce LINK and frequency C selected. *RST=POS.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_source
commands.description: Selects frequency arm source. LINK samples on edge of A or B, or external (frequency C). *RST=AUTO.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_timer
commands.description: Time sampling interval (when SOURce TIMer). *RST=50.0E-6.

commands.name: sense_frequency_arm_timer_status_query
commands.description: Returns 0 (use selected time sampling) or 1 (selected time sampling below approximate minimum sampling interval). Query only.

commands.name: sense_frequency_range_upper
commands.description: Sets the upper frequency range. 1=A, 2=B, 3=C. *RST: A=125 MHZ, B=70 MHZ, C=1.375 GHZ.

commands.name: sense_frequency_range_auto
commands.description: ONCE autoscales UPPer and LOWer for the input signal. Event with query. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_frequency_range_center_auto
commands.description: ONCE autoscales UPPer and LOWer retaining their difference. Event with query. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_frequency_range_lower
commands.description: Sets the lower frequency range. 1=A, 2=B, 3=C. *RST: A=75 MHZ, B=30 MHZ, C=625 MHZ.

commands.name: sense_function
commands.description: Selects the sensor function. Choices (short form, in quotes): FREQ[1|2|3], FREQ[1|2|3]:HIST, TINT, TINT:HIST. HIST=Fast Histogram. 1=A, 2=B, 3=C. *RST=FREQ.

commands.name: sense_histogram_accumulate
commands.description: Fast Histogram accumulate. Coupled to :CALCulate:HISTogram:ACCumulate. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_histogram_arm_level
commands.description: External arm level for Fast Histogram (SOURce EXTernal required). *RST=1.5 V.

commands.name: sense_histogram_arm_slope
commands.description: External arm slope for Fast Histogram (SOURce EXTernal required). *RST=POS.

commands.name: sense_histogram_arm_source
commands.description: Event that starts Fast Histogram acquisition. IMMediate is as fast as possible. *RST=IMMediate.

commands.name: sense_histogram_samples_above_query
commands.description: Returns the number of Fast Histogram samples above RANGe:UPPer. Query only.

commands.name: sense_histogram_samples_below_query
commands.description: Returns the number of Fast Histogram samples below RANGe:LOWer. Query only.

commands.name: sense_histogram_samples_in_query
commands.description: Returns the number of samples in the Fast Histogram. Query only.

commands.name: sense_histogram_trigger_count
commands.description: Number of frequency or TI measurements required for a Fast Histogram update. *RST=1000.

commands.name: sense_roscillator_source_query
commands.description: Returns the reference oscillator source (INT, EXT, or ERR); auto-detected. Query only.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_delay
commands.description: Main timebase delay. *RST=0.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_delay_link
commands.description: Delay reference. Coupled to SWEep:TIME:PANorama:DELay:LINK. *RST=CENTer.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_panorama_state
commands.description: Enables/disables Panorama measurements. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_panorama_delay
commands.description: Panorama timebase delay. *RST=0.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_panorama_delay_link
commands.description: Panorama delay reference. Coupled to SWEep:TIME:DELay:LINK. *RST=CENTer.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_panorama_span
commands.description: Panorama timebase span. *RST=1E-3.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_repetitive_state
commands.description: ON=Repetitive, OFF=Real time. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_repetitive_average
commands.description: Repetitive averaging; applies when SWEep:TIME:REPetitive ON. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_span
commands.description: Main timebase span. *RST=1E-3.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_atimer_query
commands.description: Returns the sample time for ARM:SOURce AUTO in vs Time or Histogram from vs Time. Query only.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_ecount
commands.description: Arm event count (SOURce INTernal[1|2] required). *RST=1.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_level
commands.description: External arm level (SOURce EXTernal required). *RST=1.5 V.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_slope
commands.description: TI arm slope. 1=A, 2=B, 3=EXT when SOURce INTernal[1|2] or EXTernal respectively. *RST=POS.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_source
commands.description: TI arm source. AUTO couples time sampling to main timebase for vs Time or Histogram from vs Time. *RST=AUTO.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_arm_timer
commands.description: Time sampling interval (when SOURce TIMer). *RST=10E-6.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_range_upper
commands.description: Sets the upper time-interval range. *RST=1.4E-3.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_range_auto
commands.description: ONCE autoscales UPPer and LOWer for the input signal. Event with query. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_range_center_auto
commands.description: ONCE autoscales UPPer and LOWer retaining their difference. Event with query. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_range_lower
commands.description: Sets the lower time-interval range. *RST=600E-9.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_route
commands.description: Time-interval input routing. *RST=SEParate.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_slope
commands.description: TI measurement slope. 1=A (TI start edge), 2=B (TI stop edge). *RST=POS.

commands.name: sense_tinterval_type
commands.description: Time-interval type. PLUS = +TI; PMINus = +/- TI. *RST=PLUS.

commands.name: status_operation_event_query
commands.description: Queries the Operation Status Event register. Query only.

commands.name: status_operation_condition_query
commands.description: Queries the Operation Status Condition register. Query only.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the Operation Status Enable register.

commands.name: status_operation_ntransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Operation Status negative transition filter.

commands.name: status_operation_ptransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Operation Status positive transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_event_query
commands.description: Queries the Questionable Status Event register. Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition_query
commands.description: Queries the Questionable Status Condition register. Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Status Enable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_ntransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Status negative transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_ptransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Status positive transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_frequency_event_query
commands.description: Queries the Questionable Frequency Status Event register. Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_frequency_condition_query
commands.description: Queries the Questionable Frequency Status Condition register. Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_frequency_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Frequency Status Enable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_frequency_ntransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Frequency Status negative transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_frequency_ptransition
commands.description: Sets or queries the Questionable Frequency Status positive transition filter.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Presets the :STATus enable and transition registers. Event; no query.

commands.name: system_clicker
commands.description: Enables/disables the audible front-panel key click. *RST=ON.

commands.name: system_error_query
commands.description: Responds with the first (oldest) error in the error queue. Query only.

commands.name: system_key
commands.description: Simulates a front-panel key press.

commands.name: system_key_log_query
commands.description: Returns the contents of the key queue and empties the queue. Query only.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Sets the analyzer to the same state as the front-panel Preset key. Event; no query.

commands.name: system_print_query
commands.description: Puts PCL-formatted display image data in the Output Queue. Query only.

commands.name: system_ssaver
commands.description: Enables or disables the screen saver capability. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: system_version_query
commands.description: Returns the SCPI version (1990.0). Query only.

commands.name: trigger_auto
commands.description: ON auto-triggers if necessary; OFF waits indefinitely for a trigger. *RST=ON.

commands.name: trigger_frequency_level
commands.description: Frequency-trigger value. 1=frequency A, 2=frequency B, 3=frequency C. Applies when SOURce LINK and measuring frequency. *RST: A=100 MHZ, B=50 MHZ, C=1 GHZ.

commands.name: trigger_frequency_level_auto
commands.description: ON couples the level to the center of FREQ:RANGe. *RST=ON.

commands.name: trigger_frequency_slope
commands.description: Triggers on rising (POS) or falling (NEG) edge of frequency vs Time. *RST=POS.

commands.name: trigger_hfreject
commands.description: High Frequency Reject. *RST=OFF.

commands.name: trigger_level
commands.description: External-trigger level (SOURce EXTernal). *RST=1.5 V.

commands.name: trigger_link_query
commands.description: Returns the string from :SENSe:FUNCtion. Query only.

commands.name: trigger_slope
commands.description: External-trigger slope (SOURce EXTernal). *RST=POS.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Trigger source. LINK = frequency or TI value triggering. *RST=LINK.

commands.name: trigger_tinterval_level
commands.description: Time-interval-trigger value. Applies when SOURce LINK and measuring TI. *RST=1E-3.

commands.name: trigger_tinterval_level_auto
commands.description: ON couples the level to the center of TINT:RANGe. *RST=ON.

commands.name: trigger_tinterval_slope
commands.description: Triggers on rising (POS) or falling (NEG) edge of TI vs Time. *RST=POS.
